title: DOM & Events
summary: The DOM tree (Document, Object, Model), handling events with onclick and addEventListener, mouseover, input changes, and toggling page UI.

examples.title: What is the DOM?
examples.description: The DOM is the Document Object Model—a tree of objects the browser builds from your HTML. Document is the whole page (document). Object means each element is a node you can access in JS. Model is the parent/child tree (html → body → p). You read or change the page with JavaScript. Change the demo paragraph id or log document.body.children.length in the script.

files.html:
Document Object Model

  • Document — the entire page (document)
  • Object — each tag is a node, e.g. document.getElementById("demo")
  • Model — tree structure: parent and child elements

Run the example — JavaScript will read this element from the document.

examples.title: JavaScript events
examples.description: An event is something that happens in the browser—a click, key press, or mouse move—and your code runs in response. Buttons, forms, and interactive pages. Add a third button that resets the count to 0.

files.html: Count: 0

examples.title: Mouseover event
examples.description: mouseover fires when the pointer enters an element; mouseout fires when it leaves. Tooltips, hover highlights, or preview effects. Change the hover message or box border color in the script.

examples.title: Input event
examples.description: Mirror text from an input field as the user types.

examples.title: Open and close a sidebar
examples.description: On mobile, sidebars are often hidden until the user taps a menu button—JavaScript toggles a CSS class to show or hide it. Click Menu to slide the sidebar open; click again to close it. Change the button label or sidebar link text.

practice.description: Add a button that toggles the background color of the page between white and light yellow.

starter.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Practice</title>
</head>
<body>
<button id="toggle">Toggle theme</button>

<!-- Js code below -->
<script>
// Add click listener on #toggle
</script>
</body>
</html>
starter.css: body { font-family: sans-serif; padding: 2rem; transition: background 0.2s; }

hints: Track on/off with a boolean variable
